title: Pronoms sujets et le verbe être
lesson: The subject pronouns are je, tu, il, elle, nous, vous, ils, elles. The verb 'être' in the present tense is conjugated: je suis, tu es, il/elle est, nous sommes, vous êtes, ils/elles sont. 'Je' becomes 'j'' before a vowel.

items.explanation: With 'je', the verb être is conjugated 'suis' (je suis).
With 'tu', the verb être is conjugated 'es' (tu es).
With 'il', the verb être is conjugated 'est' (il est).
With 'elle', the verb être is conjugated 'est' (elle est).
With 'nous', the verb être is conjugated 'sommes' (nous sommes).
With 'vous', the verb être is conjugated 'êtes' (vous êtes), with a circumflex accent.
With 'ils', the verb être is conjugated 'sont' (ils sont).
With 'elles', the verb être is conjugated 'sont' (elles sont).
The form 'es' corresponds to the pronoun 'tu' (tu es). At the start of a sentence, write 'Tu' with a capital letter.
The form 'sommes' corresponds to the pronoun 'nous' (nous sommes). At the start of a sentence, write 'Nous'.
Before a vowel, 'je' is elided to 'j'' (j'ai, j'aime). With être, you say 'je suis': the form stays 'suis'.
For a man, use the pronoun 'il' (il est). At the start of a sentence, write 'Il'.
'Je' elides to 'j'' only before a vowel (j'ai, j'aime). 'suis' begins with a consonant, so it stays 'je suis'.
